title: STTM Mapping IR
description: Normalized representation of the STTM workbook, produced by the STTM Parser specialist from the deterministic scripts/parse_sttm.py output. One instance per Generate run.

id.description: Stable row identifier assigned in workbook row order, e.g. M001. Referenced by buildspec.mapping_refs for traceability.

source_table.description: null when the mapping is a generated column with no source (transformation is GENERATED, DEFAULT, SEQUENCE, UUID, or CONSTANT) -- see scripts/parse_sttm.py, which passes a workbook row with both Source Table and Source Column blank through as null rather than failing. Required (non-null) for DIRECT, UDF, and LOOKUP.

source_column.description: A single source column, multiple when the transformation combines several columns (e.g. concatenation), or null under the same conditions as source_table -- the two are always both-null or both-populated, never just one (scripts/parse_sttm.py rejects a row with exactly one blank as malformed).

transformation.description: GENERATED, DEFAULT, SEQUENCE, and UUID are the sourceless kinds -- a target column produced without reading any source column at all (e.g. a ROW_NUMBER surrogate key, a fixed default, a database sequence, a generated UUID). NEEDS_REVIEW is set by the Mapping Resolver, never by the STTM Parser, when the workbook's own transformation note is too ambiguous to classify into any of the other kinds.

transformation_detail.description: The transformation note exactly as written in the STTM workbook, preserved verbatim for audit. Empty string if the workbook cell was blank.

udf_reference.description: Name of the UDF from udf.md this mapping invokes, or null when transformation is not UDF.
